title: Fermented Crumbly Indian Nuts
description: A show-off kerala dish featuring fermented crumbly nuts with a spicy chili sauce, suitable for nut-free diets.
instructions: Combine soy sauce, vinegar, ginger paste, and salt in a bowl. Mix well.
Add coriander seeds to the mixture and roast them lightly in a pan until fragrant.
Gradually add sesame oil while continuously stirring with a wooden spoon until all ingredients are thoroughly mixed.
Once everything is evenly combined, let it cool slightly before spreading out into thin layers on parchment paper-lined baking trays.
Sprinkle red chili powder over the mixture and mix gently to distribute evenly. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and place in a warm spot or inside an oven set at 160°F for about 8 hours.

After fermentation, break up the mixture into crumbly pieces using your hands and transfer them onto a wire rack placed over parchment paper for further air-drying. Ensure they are thoroughly dried before serving.
